steps.title: Find angle YXZ from angle sum
steps.content:
In triangle $XYZ$, angle $Y=90^\circ$ and angle $XZY=63^\circ$. Using the angle sum property, the three angles of a triangle sum to $180^\circ$:
$$\angle YXZ = 180^\circ - 90^\circ - 63^\circ = 27^\circ$$

steps.title: Use similar triangles to find angle ZPQ
steps.content:
Since $PQ$ is parallel to $XY$, triangle $ZPQ$ is similar to triangle $ZXY$ (they share angle $Z$, and the parallel condition makes the other angles match). In similar triangles, corresponding angles are equal:
$$\angle ZPQ = \angle ZXY = 27^\circ$$

steps.title: Find angle XPQ using supplementary angles
steps.content:
Point $P$ lies on segment $XZ$, so angles $ZPQ$ and $XPQ$ form a linear pair at $P$. Linear-pair angles are supplementary:
$$\angle XPQ = 180^\circ - \angle ZPQ = 180^\circ - 27^\circ = 153^\circ$$
The answer is 153.
